summaryAm: አናንሲ እና ኤሊ ስለ ፍትሕ፣ ማጋራት እና ደግ ብልሃት የሚማሩበት ታሪክ።
moralAm: ብልሃት ከፍትሕ ጋር ሲሆን ይጠቅማል፤ ጓደኛን መከበር ግን ይበልጣል።

source.name: Traditional African folktale
source.notes: Traditional/public-domain source inspiration only. This is an original child-friendly Amharic adaptation with softened themes for ages 3-6.

pages.textAm: አናንሲ በዛፍ ጥላ ሥር ምሳ አዘጋጅቶ ነበር።
pages.textEn: Anansi prepared lunch under the shade of a tree.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 1: አናንሲ በዛፍ ጥላ ሥር ምሳ አዘጋጅቶ ነበር።

pages.textAm: ኤሊው በቀስታ መጥቶ “እኔም ልካፈል?” ብሎ ጠየቀ።
pages.textEn: Turtle slowly came and asked if he could share too.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 2: ኤሊው በቀስታ መጥቶ “እኔም ልካፈል?” ብሎ ጠየቀ።

pages.textAm: አናንሲ ትንሽ ብልሃት አደረገ፣ ኤሊው ግን በጸጥታ አስተዋለ።
pages.textEn: Anansi tried a small trick, but Turtle quietly noticed.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 3: አናንሲ ትንሽ ብልሃት አደረገ፣ ኤሊው ግን በጸጥታ አስተዋለ።

pages.textAm: ኤሊው እጆቹን ለመታጠብ በቀስታ ወደ ውሃ ሄደ። አናንሲ ግን በጠረጴዛው አጠገብ ብቻውን ቆይቶ ምግቡን ተመለከተ።
pages.textEn: Turtle slowly went to wash his hands while Anansi waited alone beside the food.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 4: Turtle slowly walking to wash his hands while Anansi waits beside the lunch table.

pages.textAm: ኤሊው ሲመለስ ምግቡ ቀድሞ ተጀምሮ ነበር። አናንሲ ይህ ለጓደኛው እንደማይመች በልቡ ተሰማው።
pages.textEn: When Turtle returned, the food had already been started, and Anansi felt that this was not fair to his friend.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 5: Turtle returning to the lunch table while Anansi realizes the food was started too soon.

pages.textAm: ኤሊው አልተቆጣም። “በሌላ ቀን እኔም እጋብዝሃለሁ፤ እንግዲህ ሁለታችንም መጠበቅን እንለማመድ” አለ።
pages.textEn: Turtle did not get angry; he invited Anansi for another day so they could both practice waiting.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Child-friendly warm storybook illustration for Anansi and the Turtle, page 6: calm Turtle kindly inviting Anansi for another day after the lunch table mix-up.

pages.textAm: በሌላ ቀን አናንሲ ወደ ውሃ ዳር መጣ። ኤሊው ምግቡን በቅጠል ላይ አዘጋጀ እና “ጓደኛ ሲመጣ እንጠብቃለን” አለ።
pages.textEn: Another day, Anansi came to the water’s edge, where Turtle prepared food and said friends wait for each other.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Anansi arriving at the water's edge while Turtle prepares food on a leaf and speaks about waiting for friends.

pages.textAm: አናንሲ ወደ ውሃ መቅረብ እንደማይመቸው ተናገረ። ኤሊውም “ሁላችንም የራሳችን መንገድ አለን” ብሎ በደግነት መለሰ።
pages.textEn: Anansi said the water did not feel easy for him, and Turtle kindly said everyone has their own way.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Calm Turtle speaking kindly with Anansi at the water's edge about everyone having their own way.

pages.textAm: ኤሊው ምግቡን ወደ ዛፉ ጥላ ትንሽ አቀረበ። አናንሲም ከውሃ ሳይገባ ከጓደኛው ጋር መብላት ቻለ።
pages.textEn: Turtle moved the food a little closer to the tree shade so Anansi could share without going into the water.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Turtle moving food on a leaf toward tree shade so Anansi can share comfortably near the water.

pages.textAm: አናንሲ ሁሉም ጓደኛ በተመቸው ቦታ መካፈል እንደሚችል ተረዳ። ኤሊውም “ፍትሕ ማለት ሁሉንም መጠበቅ ነው” አለ።
pages.textEn: Anansi learned that friends can share in a place that works for everyone, and Turtle said fairness means waiting for all.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Anansi and Turtle near a pond learning that fair sharing means waiting for everyone.

pages.textAm: አናንሲ ይቅርታ ጠይቆ ምግቡን በእኩል አካፈለ። ኤሊውም “ብልሃት ከፍትሕ ጋር ሲሆን ያምራል” አለ።
pages.textEn: Anansi apologized and shared equally, learning that cleverness is best with fairness.
pages.illustrationPrompt: Anansi and Turtle sharing food equally under a tree near water.
